question: Opening Hook: Build hype for the launch happening TOMORROW (Jan 29).
text: Guys, get your wallets ready! TOMORROW night, Jan 29th at 10 PM, the chaos begins. The Gintama 'Welcome to Yorozuya' series is finally dropping. If you love satire, samurais, and aliens, this is the chaotic masterpiece you need.

question: How do you introduce the main character Gintoki?
hint: Perm / Sugar Addict / Dead Fish Eyes
text: Here is the man himself, Sakata Gintoki. Silver perm, dead fish eyes, and a serious sugar addiction. He's holding his legendary wooden sword 'Lake Toya'. He looks lazy, but he is the soul of the Samurai spirit and actually quite dependable when things get real.

question: Use the 'Glasses' running joke to engage the chat.
hint: The Glasses Are The Human / Stand
text: Next up is Shinpachi! But guys, be honest... are we pulling for the boy or the glasses? In the anime, the glasses are 95% of his character and the human is just the stand. Who wants the glasses? Type 'Glasses' in the chat!

question: Describe the contrast between Kagura's looks and her strength.
text: Look at the cute space buns! But don't be fooled. Kagura is from the Yato Clan—she is literally the muscle of the group. She's holding her bulletproof umbrella. She's brutal, hungry, and absolutely adorable.

question: Highlight the specific accessory detail for the Vice-Commander.
text: The Demon Vice-Commander, Hijikata. He looks so cool and tough, but look closer at his hand... yes, that is a bottle of Mayonnaise. He puts mayo on everything. The detail on the bottle is hilarious.

question: Teach the chat the famous catchphrase for Katsura.
text: If you pull this long-haired beauty, you are legally required to say the line. I'll say 'Zura', you say... 'It's not Zura, it's Katsura!' He even comes with his mysterious duck-pet, Elizabeth.

question: Describe the scale and cuteness of the mascot.
text: Every team needs a mascot. This is Sadaharu. He is massive, fluffy, and white. His favorite hobby is biting Gintoki's head. If you love pet figures, this one is heavy and super detailed.

question: Closing: Explain the 'Secret' rarity and Box Format.
text: There are 12 basic characters to collect, including the Shinsengumi and the Revolutionaries. But the Secret? It's a 1 in 144 chance. If you want the full chaos of Edo, grab the whole box so you don't get duplicates!

question: Pitch to Die-Hard Gintama Fans: Tap into the 'Silver Soul' emotion.
text: To my Gintama family: You know this isn't just a comedy. We laughed till we cried, and then we cried till we laughed. This set is the spirit of Yorozuya. Putting Gintoki, Kagura, and Shinpachi together on your shelf... it's like keeping that Silver Soul eternal. You cannot separate the trio!

question: Pitch to Die-Hard Gintama Fans: Use inside jokes (Mayo/Zura).
text: Only true fans understand why a cool samurai is holding a bottle of Mayonnaise. Or why we scream 'It's not Zura!' These figures are loaded with Easter eggs that only WE understand. It's a badge of honor to have these on your desk.

question: Pitch to General Anime Fans: Sell the unique 'Sci-Fi Samurai' aesthetic.
text: Even if you haven't watched all 300 episodes, look at the aesthetic. It's Edo-period Japan meets Sci-Fi Aliens. You have traditional Kimonos mixed with Bazookas and Motorcycles. It is the most stylish, chaotic design in Shonen history. It fits perfectly in any anime collection.

question: Pitch to General Anime Fans: Highlight the 'Chaotic Masterpiece' reputation.
text: You are looking at the King of Comedy Anime. This show parodied Dragon Ball, One Piece, and everything else. Owning these figures is owning a piece of anime history. The character designs are iconic—especially Sadaharu the giant dog.

question: Pitch to POP MART Collectors: Focus on Detail & Quality (ignore lore).
text: For my collectors who just want high quality: Look at the sculpting details. The gradient paint on Gintoki's hair, the distinct accessories like the Smoking Pipe and the Umbrella. POP MART really leveled up the PVC quality here. They feel heavy and premium, not hollow.

question: Pitch to POP MART Collectors: Focus on Rarity & The Secret.
text: Let's talk numbers. The Secret Chaser is Takasugi, and the ratio is 1:144. That is extremely hard to pull. If you are an investment collector, you know a 1/144 IP figure holds value. Do not sleep on the chance to pull the Secret from a whole box.
